authorities.name: California Department of Fish and Wildlife

sources.title: 14 CCR §7.50(b)(18) — Burney Creek
sources.quotedText: (18) Burney Creek (Shasta Co.) from Burney Creek Falls downstream to Lake Britton. All year. Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used. 0 trout

sources.title: 14 CCR §7.50(a) — General provisions for the special-regulations trout water list
sources.quotedText: (3) Daily bag and possession limits, unless otherwise noted, mean the total number of trout. (4) Unless otherwise provided, it is unlawful to possess more than one daily bag limit.

sources.title: McArthur-Burney Falls Memorial State Park — CA State Parks
California Fish and Game Code §7145 — Sport Fishing License Requirement
sources.quotedText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...

sources.title: USGS GNIS (The National Map Gazetteer) — Streams layer record for gaz_id 220202, "Burney Creek", Stream, Shasta Co., CA
sources.quotedText: {"gaz_id":220202,"gaz_name":"Burney Creek","gaz_featureclass":"Stream","fcode":46000,"state_alpha":"CA","county_name":"Shasta"}; geometry multipoint, first point (Web Mercator -13542665.5182, 5015480.5175) converts to lon -121.655834, lat 41.021277 — ~1.2km from Burney Falls (GNIS 257711, cited by Wikipedia's {{cite gnis|257711}} at 41.0107162, -121.6527649) and consistent with Wikipedia's description of Burney Creek's mouth on Lake Britton "to the north" of the falls; ~1.33km from the file's previously recorded coordinate (-121.6420, 41.0155), which was an unsourced estimate — coordinate corrected to the GNIS point. (A second, more distant point pair in the same multipoint geometry, ~14-18km further south, does not match this water's known short course near Burney Falls and appears to be an unrelated same-named GNIS entry; disregarded.)

regulations.humanSummary: Open all year, catch-and-release only, on the segment from Burney Creek Falls downstream to Lake Britton.
regulations.verbatimText: Burney Creek (Shasta Co.) from Burney Creek Falls downstream to Lake Britton. All year.

regulations.humanSummary: 0 trout — catch-and-release only, from Burney Creek Falls downstream to Lake Britton.
Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used.
regulations.verbatimText: Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used.

parameters.note: Anglers 16 years of age or older must have a valid California sport fishing license.

regulations.humanSummary: California sport fishing license required for anglers 16+
regulations.verbatimText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...
